title.purpose: Panel headers, section labels, sidebar group headings.

body.purpose: Nav labels, list items, form text. Max 65–75ch for prose.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Floating submenu panels. Signals transient overlay above the layout.

shadows.name: raised-rest
shadows.purpose: .raised controls at rest. Creates the cartoony black bottom edge.

shadows.name: raised-hover
shadows.purpose: .raised controls on hover. Deepens 2px as the control lifts 2px.

shadows.name: raised-active
shadows.purpose: .raised controls when pressed. Shadow collapses as control presses down.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Button press and box-shadow transitions on .raised controls.

motion.name: spring-nav
motion.purpose: Nav highlight layoutId animation. The only spring in the system — physically follows cursor.

components.name: Primary Button (Raised)
components.description: Primary action button with the cartoony raised/pressed interaction. The signature MetricYak control style.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; background-color: #d9591a; color: #fafaf8; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: 1.5px solid #0f0e0d; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 0 #0f0e0d; transform: translateY(0); transition: transform 100ms ease, box-shadow 100ms ease; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover:not(:active):not(:disabled) { box-shadow: 0 6px 0 0 #0f0e0d; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:active:not(:disabled) { box-shadow: none; transform: translateY(4px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9591a; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; box-shadow: 0 2px 0 0 #0f0e0d; }

components.name: Secondary Button (Raised)
components.description: Secondary action. White fill, Ink border, same raised press mechanic.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; background-color: #ffffff; color: #1a1917; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: 1.5px solid #0f0e0d; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 0 #0f0e0d; transform: translateY(0); transition: transform 100ms ease, box-shadow 100ms ease; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; outline: none; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover:not(:active):not(:disabled) { box-shadow: 0 6px 0 0 #0f0e0d; transform: translateY(-2px); background-color: #f5f4f0; } .ds-btn-secondary:active:not(:disabled) { box-shadow: none; transform: translateY(4px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9591a; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; box-shadow: 0 2px 0 0 #0f0e0d; }

components.name: Nav Item (Default)
components.description: Standard navigation rail item. Flat — does not use .raised.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { position: relative; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; width: 100%; padding: 0.5rem; background: transparent; color: #1a1917; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease; text-align: left; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background-color: #d4d0c8; } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9591a; outline-offset: -2px; }

components.name: Nav Item (Active)
components.description: Active navigation rail item with Yak Orange highlight. Flat — does not use .raised.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { position: relative; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; width: 100%; padding: 0.5rem; background: transparent; color: #1a1917; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease; text-align: left; } .ds-nav-item--active { background-color: rgba(217, 89, 26, 0.15); border: 1px solid #d9591a; font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Settings panel search. Transparent fill, border shifts to Yak Orange on focus. Flat — no .raised.
components.css: .ds-search-wrap { position: relative; } .ds-search-icon { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 0.625rem; transform: translateY(-50%); pointer-events: none; color: #8c8880; } .ds-search-input { width: 100%; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem 0.5rem 2rem; background: transparent; color: #1a1917; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; border: 1px solid #d4d0c8; border-radius: 0.5rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-search-input::placeholder { color: #8c8880; } .ds-search-input:focus { border-color: #d9591a; }

narrative.overview:
MetricYak's interface is a well-calibrated tool you actually enjoy picking up. Like a good instrument — a quality scale, a precision gauge, a reliable meter — it's honest about what it is, quick to respond, and quietly satisfying to use. The personality isn't layered on top of the function; it's baked into the craft: the warmth in the orange, the give in the rounded corners, the tactile press of a raised button.

The surface serves the data. Metric values, event streams, monitor states — these are the signal, and the UI's job is to get out of the way. Quirk and warmth live in the margins: a clever label when a list is empty, a loading state with personality, the physical satisfaction of a cartoony button press. On the metric canvas itself, the rule is restraint.
keyCharacteristics: Single saturated accent (Yak Orange) kept deliberately rare against warm neutral surfaces
Cartoony raised interaction style on controls — tactile, physical, memorable
System font at a fixed readable scale — no display pairing, no fluid sizing
Flat by default; tonal sidebar layer creates depth without shadows
Motion serves state only: panel slide, accordion reveal, nav highlight spring, button press
Personality lives in the margins: empty states, microcopy, transitions

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Yak Orange appears on ≤10% of any given screen. It marks the active item, the primary action, the focus ring. Used more broadly, it becomes noise.

rules.name: The Tonal Layer Rule
rules.body: Sidebar Ash (light) and Near-Black Surface (dark) are the only depth mechanism at rest. No shadows on flat surfaces — only on transient overlays.

rules.name: The Ink Rule
rules.body: Ink (#0f0e0d) is reserved for the raised interaction shadow/border. It should not appear as a general UI color elsewhere, which keeps the pressed-control effect feeling distinct and physical.

rules.name: The Fixed Scale Rule
rules.body: No clamp() sizing on product UI. Fixed rem values throughout.

rules.name: The One-Weight-per-Role Rule
rules.body: Each typographic role has one weight. Use label hierarchy or color for emphasis — not mid-paragraph bold.

rules.name: The Flat-First Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. If you're reaching for a shadow on a card or section, use a tonal background shift instead. The only structural shadows are on overlays and raised controls.

dos: Do keep Yak Orange on ≤10% of any screen surface — it marks what matters.
Do use .raised on primary action buttons and prominent form controls for tactile character. It is an opt-in, not the default for every element.
Do use tonal backgrounds (Sidebar Ash / Near-Black Surface) for spatial depth. Shadows are for overlays and raised controls only.
Do let personality show in empty states, loading copy, and microcopy. The metric canvas stays clean.
Do use rounded-md (8px) consistently across all interactive controls.
Do animate state, not decoration — panel slides, accordion reveals, nav highlight, button press.
Do give every interactive component default, hover, focus, active, and disabled states before shipping.
Do use skeleton loaders for async content, not spinners in the middle of content areas.
Do cap prose and settings content at max-w-2xl (65–75ch).
donts: Don't make it feel like Datadog or Splunk — dense enterprise dashboards, aggressive blue/purple, wall-to-wall charts.
Don't make it feel like a generic SaaS tool (Notion, Monday.com) — soft pastels, friendly illustrations on every screen.
Don't make it feel like Grafana OSS — every feature exposed at once, low polish, assembled rather than designed.
Don't make it feel like a corporate BI tool (Tableau, Power BI) — heavy, formal, built for presentations.
Don't apply .raised to every control — nav items, accordion triggers, and sidebar list items stay flat.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on list items or cards.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Emphasis goes through weight or size.
Don't use display or editorial fonts for UI labels, button text, or data values. System sans only.
Don't add decorative motion. Every animation must communicate a state change.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer to any UX problem.
Don't crowd the metric canvas — if an element doesn't help the user understand their data faster, it loses.
